steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage explains how caffeic acid combats free radicals in mushrooms, then extends that finding to humans. The transition word must signal addition — the human benefit builds on, rather than contrasts with, the mushroom finding.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — For example,: signals an example, but the human case is not an example of the mushroom case — it's a related extension. ✗
  • Choice B — Moreover,: means 'furthermore' or 'in addition,' correctly extending the caffeic acid benefit to a new context. ✓
  • Choice C — Conversely,: signals opposition, but the human and mushroom findings align rather than conflict. ✗
  • Choice D — Rather,: signals replacement or substitution, which misrepresents the relationship between the two findings. ✗
That is the correct answer.
